reviews.review_description: For me, this is the best stand alone helicopter simulator I’ve found on the Quest, and flying around familiar places on Google Earth with buildings added on top is great. Haven’t really got in to the two fixed wing aircraft.

This game is the only standard alone flight game powered by google that even holds up to the standard. Dawn of jets is great too, so is flight 75 but no other stand alone game is better. Sure the graphics could be better but that's about it. I wasted money on flight unlimited and it just ran me back to this game. If you want to just cruise and enjoy flying this is it!
Please advise me what to do

Awful. Kvetched. Ghastly. Wretched.

Just like the other reviewer said. Plane will not work or take off for love or money. Worst aviation app ever experienced. It's reach exceeds its grasp. Obfuscating, lugubrious, profoundly awful.

Granted, I am on low to medium speed internet. I am on the free government provided internet. In awhile, I will upgrade to high speed internet, again. YouTube VR app also suffers whenever and where ever there is paucity of broadband.
When a person goes on to the next phase of life, think of it as getting a broadband upgrade. It isn't a different set of physics. It's the same but faster. Walking becomes teleportation, talking becomes telepathy. Music is experienced in over a hundred octaves. Sight is experienced with over 100 primary colors only three of which humans can experience while on Earth. 
There is a new time dynamic as the Einstein all things are happening at the same time because linear time is an illusion, subjective while time travel is time experienced on an objective level. It is this new elevated time dynamic where linear time is somewhat suspended and irrelevant that the next phase of life experiences. 
Linear time without time travel would have no definition. 
Time travel without linear time would have no structure. 
The two are essential to one another. 
Anyways, while life on this Earth can be likened to 5G broadband, the afterlife is like 1T broadband. The dreamworld is about 50G PON position optical network broadband. 

The whole point is I'm getting an upgraded internet in a few days if all goes well. Then I can experience World Flight VR with all its features running on all 12 cylinders. I will then rewrite the review. 
I played Flight Unlimited VR. That app works really good with little to even no internet connection. Flight Unlimited is better. Whoever wrote that this app is better than Flight Unlimited are shills. 


To be fair, I will return when I get this running on high speed internet with a new cable company provided modem. This app could turn out to be better than a lot of other VR apps. The plane doesn't look as good as planes on all other VR apps except for Simple Planes. What drew me in was the variety of locations around the planet photorealistic and accurate rendering from Google Earth. I wanted to fly over the airport in my town. No dice. And changing the weather and time of year. Profound. 

Volveré. Seguro.

Update: Go into Mission. The first mission is to land at an airport in Tokyo. I opted to not do that and just fly free around Tokyo. It is a very exemplary and exceptional VR app. The plane isn't as rock star as the planes in Flight Unlimited or Dawn of Jets but there is the variety of scenes etc but nothing you couldn't get from Wander VR and you're even flying but not in an airplane.
This is a very good app and when I get my new upgraded internet modem, I'll tell you if there is a difference.


Another updste: 9 days later. I got my high speed internet back again. 
WF runs very good. Purrrs like a cat. Runs on all 6 cylinders.
The trick is upon take off, remember to lower the flaps even before pushing the throttle, and wait until the plane reaches full speed and pull back just a tiny bit until you see the plane lifts off clear of the runway. Pulling the sidestick back too much, rookie mistake causes the tail to touch the ground and explosion. It works really good now. The towns don't look accurate. My town, 3/10 in terms of depiction of accuracy but that's a lot better than 0/10. 


Wrap the strap of a VR hand grip around a flashlight and place the flashlight beneath the legs while sitting. This is a more structurally accurate approach.


On high speed internet this runs on all 12 cylinders. I feel remorse for what I wrote earlier. This is a Sterling platinum Dom Perignon champagne class VR app. Profound and wonderful. 
I flew over my town. Very accurate display on the screen inside the plane.

I purchased this game over the summer.  It kept crashing about 5 seconds after it opened.  Meta rep tried to help but im not tech savy so i gave up and he gave me a refund. I bought it again hoping it would work  but i still got sone kind of authentication error and it would crash. I never asked for a refund that time...i just cut my losses. Lastcweek i got a new internet service and new router. World Flight now works flawlessly. I am 100 percent happy with it now.

I have had this app for a long time and liked it but now when I try to enter it says there is an  update that has to be done.  When I try to update I don't see and update function except having g to purchase it again!

Excellent choice as there is a notable lack of genuine flight simulators on quest this is my go to flight simulator highly recommend 

My wishlist
Boeing 737n

Introduction of Google earth tiles as the procedural generated buildings can take you out of the immersion

This game is full of bugs glitches and just poor all round development it should not be for sale on the meta store under any circumstances I'm actually offended by this game. It is not finished the game will glitch when spawn on the runway a gaping hole will appear and your plane falls in to it and through the matrix, landing the plane is an absolute joke it's so bad I've managed about 5 landings in so many no matter what you explode the moment you make contact with the floor, the controls have a huge dead one leading to an awkward flight and the runways are not flat there anything but such a shame is I liked the idea and concept the development team must fix or remove from the store
Update, I've since changed to 3 stars as the helicopter actually is OK to fly so as helicopter simulator it's good

reviews.review_title: High potential but in need of more work
reviews.review_description:
If only this game had at least one commercial airliner such as Airbus A330 or even a jumbo jet 747, it would make (with a better interactive cockpit) a must have simulation on autonomous vr headset. 

Keep going on guys, I hope some future update such as tower control interaction and a better flight modele will come. 🤞
Just think it’s a waste of money ..should get more into serious flightsims

Nine out of ten times I've tried to play the game, it opens then immediately closes with an error about credentials. What's up!? It seems it may be fun to play j the one time I got it to stay open (have no idea how I did that). At this point, it's useless.

Ig I could give it zero stars I would. It's so bad that I'm packing my headset up and returning it because there is obviously zero quality control over at Meta. My review of the sim? It doesn't work. Straight up. I tried a.dozen times to take off and can't make it happen. The jet won't yaw, so that's useless. Pulling back on the stick does nadda. It's just straight up awful. All I want is to fly some planes in VR. Again, my headset is going back. I expected WAY better for $500.

First off, to quote the Rocketeer, "I like it!"

I just started, but I'm having fun.  I wish there would be a tutorial of some type, but I got the gist by watching a few YouTube videos.

It's not MSFS, nor does it claim to be, but I do think it has potential.

That having been said, and in spite of the 5 stars, I ran into an odd issue and, since I cannot find a way to connect to support, I'm posting it here.

When selecting Blue Ridge Regional Airport (KMTV) the airplane immediately crashes as soon as the runway scene begins.

My equipment is Meta Quest 3s (256GB).

I have only tried two airports, the other being Johnston County Regional Airport (KJNX), which works great and is the basis for this review (and support ticket).

reviews.review_title: It could be so good and unique on the Q3
reviews.review_description:
I like the idea behind the game, it's incredible to have a flight simulation on the Quest Standalone that theoretically allows you to fly around the world based on satellite images. This simulator has so much potential and it would be really sad if this was another game in the Quest library that was abandoned and buried by its developers instead of using the full potential of Quest 3 right now...

What really gives me a headache,  is the Controls from the aircraft itself. 
The Flightstick has a dead zone that is far too large, the Rudders are far too sensitive and the 
Throttle is a thing in itself...
Either you give it full throttle or you fall like a stone out of the sky, in between there is nothing, Glideing at slow speed is almost impossible. There is also still a lot of room for improvement in the graphics in the cockpit and on the aircraft itself. I really hope that this unique simulation will be further developed on the Quest 3. ..
Very realistic and challenging
It isn’t possible to land but it is probably just me cause I’m not very good. Still a fun game.

It's an OK sim. Not a great purchase.
Displeased that no ILS approaches in weather.
Cant make your flight route its always direct to the airport
Autopilot you can not adjust
The map is useless cause it doesn't work half the time.
Alot of updates need to be done
Like the game would like more helicopters to choose from and jets please
Has huge potential. I’m an infinite flight user and like to play aviation all the time. I think this game has HUGE potential and a good investment boost would have a tremendous impact on the game
Despite the fact I love this game I feel like there should be cockpit lights for nighttime flights
From the description, I thought this would allow you to actually see places you were flying over. I don't see the point of choosing anything on the map if it isn't true to what you see

reviews.review_title: Best VR flight sim for realism
reviews.review_description:
I bought this to try out as non of the quest flight sims have realism in terms of engine failure mechanics etc. This game is fantastic with the options to fly from basically any airport with real Google Earth type terrain and very fun for the money.

It needs some more developing as I would say it is currently only “beta” quality, I would like to see updated aircraft with a bigger range and different instrument clusters to make the game more varied. 

An online option would be amazing even if it just for 1-4 players so you can fly places with friends but as long as the developers are still working on this and don’t abandon it like 99% of VR game devs seem to do, then this could be potentially the best flight sim for quest imho.

reviews.review_title: Very good start to a sim
reviews.review_description:
Very solid start. The Warthog inclusion made me giggle like an idiot. This is a good mechanical start to a standalone flight simulator. I think this deserves recognition as a Beta test more than a full release.

Pros: 
Solid start to a VR offering on Quest. Quest 3 has some OK graphic updates. Flying a hog is fun. Real world environments are cool. 

Cons: 
Graphics of ground objects are more akin to Flight Simulator 2000 than anything a modern PCVR platform can offer, which is expected with the limitations of a quest headset, but I feel like this could still be vastly improved on the Quest 3.

Flight surfaces are not all “there.” 

Radar is weird. 

No commercial planes.

IFR and ILS are not there. 

Autopilot is bugged.

Mountain air strips have mountain features (aka not flat)

No multiplayer.

Sound design is less than immersive.

No brrrrt. 

Canyons (such as the Black Canyon of the Gunnison) are endless chasms into the depths of the earth’s mantle, deeper than the Marianas Trench.
I love this simulator and also would love to see a F-16 or F-18 added.

Looked forward to this.
But what a let down,poor graphics outside the plane,lacking detail.
Controls for me were a bit finicky where other games like for example Flight74 ,Warplanes do it better.
If it wasn't so late looking at the game when I downloaded I would of asked for a refund as I expected better,but as I was too late I just uninstall it.
I'm sure some will love it and fair play to you,enjoy,but I expect better.
Sorry DEVELOPER but not for me,you need to raise your game.

It's really a good simulator for Meta VR. The full control you have when in the cockpit to real places on the map. They even had the military bunkers that are located near my home on point. I hear some people talk trash about this game, but it's like, what do you expect? This game is totally fun and well worth every cent. It could only get better as new updates come out. A Beoing commercial liner would be absolutely cool, where you can actually view from a passenger seat, but heck, you guys did a great job! We are all waiting to see what's in store for later updates. Thanks for the great flight sim!

Good work but needs a lot more improvements. 
No updates for monthes. 

When comes the next updates / improvements ???

Update - Nov. 22.  Wow!  This dev is awesome!  He just released a new update, V 1.3, that improves the draw distance, meaning the white haze is now further in the distance and much less distracting.  He's also included new mission challenges, 2 for the aircraft and 1 for the helicopter, which are great for those that want to try and place on the leaderboards.  There have also been other tweaks and updates since release that are just adding to the overall enjoyment of this game.  This game is not only worth every penny, but should also be directly available to purchase on the Oculus store.  🙏

Original review:  Being able to fly anywhere in the world is definitely the main selling point of this game.  The level of detail is nowhere near the standards of MSFS, but for a standalone Quest 2 game, it's more than acceptable.  I wish the white haze in the distance was less noticeable at times, but I also realize the dev is probably pushing the limits of what the Q2 is capable of once your location is loaded.  Speaking of which, I'm surprised at how fast the game loads the location you choose.  

Overall, the game runs very smoothly with just a few minor hiccups in framerate in larger cities, and it's great having the choice to fly a helicopter, giving you more time to enjoy the scenery at a slower speed.  Hopefully the dev continues to work on this game, possibly adding more aircraft to choose from, and maybe being able to tweak the level of detail even further.  For the price though, I highly recommend it for those looking for a relaxing time flying anywhere in the world.  Please stick with it dev.  👍

It is remarkable how the developer has squeezed the world map and flight mechanics to run natively on Quest 2 Creating an authentic flight simulator.

My only request is that the flight model for the plane can be refined. The yoke controls seem oversensitive and less realistic.￼ Maybe an assist option could be added where the plane will naturally tend to correct itself for level flight, while still processing input from the yoke, creating a smoother flight and less jarring experience.￼

EDIT Since the last update the plane flight controls are much smoother and realistic. Thankyou.

reviews.review_title: Faster internet seems to have fixed it
reviews.review_description:
So went and upgraded my internet speed and it works great still has issue if my kids are stealing my wifi but that's ok can still play when they are off it loved flying over my town and home Thank you for the quick response and willingness to help with the issue

Couple things I'm noticing having played alot 

First some of the ground loads uneven when this happens on a run way you either run into the raised ground or ramp off the higher portion also any way to improve forward visibility by lowering the dash also in my area near st joe mo the Rosecrans memorial airport has 3d buildings on the runway for some reason also not sure if it's just me or the flight physics but on final approach I lower the flaps and star to throttle back but even the slightest bit of throttle back seems to make the plane want to drop faster then it feels like it should otherwise you end up landing at a higher speed also the ability to apply breaks as needed for shorter runways and just general stopping aside from just throwing on the parking break I've also noticed that due to the draw distance being relatively close it makes runway acquisition and line up for final approach a bit of a guessing game during visual flight maybe an indicator to assist with the line up to use as a guide I know that's not accurate or having a indicator light inside the cockpit that lights up when lined up with the runway when you are within so many miles otherwise still loving this game the new update allowing use of real time and weather is nice please keep up the amazing work on this game 

UPDATE NOV 1 2022

so this morning came a new update and it's fantastic the dash has been lowered and the cockpit seems more cozy all the controls are right there the artificial horizon was moved to the left side of the radar screen the controls seem more responsive and it seems to track better when switching between throttle flaps etc so the cockpit is so much more user friendly was doing several take offs landings with ease the throttle also seems better like it isn't as sensitive maybe I'm just getting used to it lol the graphics seems more crisp and the terrain seems more detailed fantastic job devs  also not sure if this was originally there or not but when a way point is in place auto pilot not only maintains altitude and speed and heading but it moved the plane right on path with my way point this game just gets better and better still have the buildings on the runways at Rosecrans memorial airport near st joseph mo it looks like the game is mistaking the thick white lines on the runway as buildings but with the better view it's easier to land and avoid them atleast keep up the amazing work and thank you for such an amazing game so far
Just getting started but I can't seem to avoid crash landing in the plane. Gear js down, slow throttle, flaps but boom every time. Still having fun. Any tips?

World Flight Discord - https://discord.gg/mBRJXt9D 

Great Place to see content for the game https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ia-7lFUmLQQ

I played this game upon release and loved it! After reading one pilots comments and multiple edits I decided to try the update and I am blown away. The devs took the feedback into consideration and actually put them in the sim and made it so  much better. The Radar, the auto-pilot, and the dark map are awesome. Can't wait tot see what is to come.
For what it is.. Absolutely brilliant and well worth the time playing. I would love to see the future updates and mprovements maybe more aircrafts to fly also. Best one yet.
When i click take off i have endless loading screen!!!! I Piad for this game Please help!!!

developer_response.body: Hello there, thank you for purchasing the game. I am currently working on a fix for your issue. If you are still experiencing the infinite loading problem, please send me an email at 0g.zer0gravitygames@gmail.com with the email linked to your Oculus account so I can add you to the beta version channel.

reviews.review_description: This is a great game, a lot better than I thought! But the only take away is the buildings, the 3D structures. The buildings are not what they look like and they are just cube set on the ground, that’s how I look at it. For example, Daytona International Speedway, the grandstands are just buildings. They look nothing like the real grandstands. If the building could look more realistic that would make the game so much better. But overall, Great game!

Positives
- scenery, flew from same airport as I actually fly from and could use visual clues for circuit, you can practice flying visually to a point. 
- graphics are pretty good, heli seems better. 
- user interface is great, easy to pick airports etc..

Negatives
- plane physics quite a bit off, needs a lot of airspeed to get airborne, has a very narrow operating window before it starts to stall (heli feels better but limited experience real world to flying rotary)
- could do with more instrumentation, better DI, vertical airspeed, turn and slip… 

Overall this is an amazing starting point that could progress into a really good sim.
Love the game but can there be more planes for the Prise it’s good but ye do need a bit more planes

reviews.review_title: Fun but needs work on it
reviews.review_description: I love flying around seeing the famous sighting like Eiffel Tower and Uluṟu e.t.c but the map lags out abit and whenever I try land the plane or lift off it tends to explode for no reason. overall still fun to fly around.

Love everything about this!
Flying either a helicopter or plane around my local area is great.

Edit: I purchased this app as soon as it was released and the amount of amazing updates that has been added since is astounding!!!!

I love the new detailed radar map and other great additions 👍

The only other thing that I would like added (but I can live without) is the option to invert the left joystick helicopter collective control (forwards = down).

Quest 2 developer of the year award ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

I get to the main screen where I can pick the plane or the helicopter, set all the different parameters and hit takeoff, comes to a new screen that says loading and blue line going across gets about 98% filled and doesn’t go any further for whatever reason and I let it sit there for 45 minutes and nothing happens. I’m on an Oculus 2 VR headset.. Is there a cliche? Or what? Fix please
